summary_ja: Raw backlog ingest — 5件中5件skip。Notion Token Town podcast 2件（既にエンティティページ収録済み）、Simon Tathamコルーチン記事 2件（純粋C/C++技術）、APOSD vs Clean Code討論 1件（一般ソフトウェア設計）。AI/LLM/Agent関連コンテンツなし。

decisions.title: Notion's Token Town: 5 Rebuilds, 100+ Tools, MCP vs CLIs and the Software Factory Future - Simon Last & Sarah Sachs of Notion
decisions.reason_ja: 既にentities/notion.md、entities/simon-last.md、entities/sarah-sachs.mdに全コンテンツ収録済み。同一ポッドキャストエピソードの別追跡URL。
decisions.body_excerpt: Sarah Sachs and Simon Last of Notion join us for a deep dive into how Notion built Custom Agents, why it took years and multiple rebuilds to get right, and what it means to turn a productivity tool into an agent-native system of record for enterprise work.

decisions.title: Notion's Token Town: 5 Rebuilds, 100+ Tools, MCP vs CLIs and the Software Factory Future - Simon Last & Sarah Sachs of Notion
decisions.reason_ja: 既にentities/notion.md、entities/simon-last.md、entities/sarah-sachs.mdに全コンテンツ収録済み。同一ポッドキャストエピソードの別追跡URL（redirectパラメータ違い）。
decisions.body_excerpt: For all those who missed out on London, see you in Miami next week! Notion, the knowledge work decacorn, has been building AI tooling since before ChatGPT...

decisions.title: Philosophy of coroutines
decisions.reason_ja: Simon Tathamによるコルーチンの哲学的考察。純粋なC/C++プログラミング技術記事。LLM/Agent/AI関連性なし。
decisions.body_excerpt: I've been a huge fan of coroutines since the mid-1990s. I encountered the idea as a student, when I first read about them...

decisions.title: Writing custom C++20 coroutine systems
decisions.reason_ja: C++20コルーチンシステムの実装詳細。純粋なプログラミング言語技術記事。LLM/Agent/AI関連性なし。
decisions.body_excerpt: C++20's coroutine system has a lot of flexibility that Python's generators (for example) don't. But the flip side of that flexibility is that you have to do a lot of work if you want to use it...

decisions.title: A Philosophy of Software Design vs Clean Code
decisions.reason_ja: John OusterhoutとRobert Martinのソフトウェア設計哲学議論。一般的なコード設計原則（メソッド長、コメント、TDD）に関する討論。LLM/Agent/AI文脈なし。
decisions.body_excerpt: This document is the result of a series of discussions, some online and some in person, held between Robert "Uncle Bob" Martin and John Ousterhout between September 2024 and February 2025...
